source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
As seen by an inertial observer $O$, photons of frequency $
u$ are incident, at an angle $	heta_{i}$ to the normal, on a plane mirror. These photons are reflected back at an angle $	heta_{r}$ to the normal and at a frequency $
u^{\prime}$ as shown in Fig. 3.10. Find $	heta_{r}$ and $
u^{\prime}$ in terms of $	heta_{i}$ and $
u$ if the mirror is moving in the $x$ direction with velocity $v$ relative to $O$.
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image string representation rather than a file path or URL

asset_registry_record shows one valid image asset exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: question asks for two outputs, theta_r and nu', rather than a single scalar result
the setup combines incident angle, reflected angle, frequency shift, and a moving mirror, which indicates multi-condition relativistic reasoning
answer_text contains two nontrivial closed-form expressions, suggesting a multi-step derivation rather than direct lookup
verifiability: answer is given as explicit symbolic formulas for both requested quantities
answer_type is set/short_text with concrete expressions that are amenable to symbolic or rule-based checking
question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true support downstream validation

decision_reasons: question, answer, and one valid image asset are all present, so the sample has the core semantic assets needed for recovery and verification
initial scoring is strong on image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
the recorded issue is limited to metadata.image_paths formatting, while the registry still passed and the image manifest shows an existing PNG asset

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text:
As seen by an inertial observer $O$, photons of frequency $
u$ are incident, at an angle $	heta_{i}$ to the normal, on a plane mirror. These photons are reflected back at an angle $	heta_{r}$ to the normal and at a frequency $
u^{\prime}$ as shown in Fig. 3.10. Find $	heta_{r}$ and $
u^{\prime}$ in terms of $	heta_{i}$ and $
u$ if the mirror is moving in the $x$ direction with velocity $v$ relative to $O$.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image string representation rather than a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain:
Solution:
Let $\Sigma, \Sigma^{\prime}$ be the rest frames of the observer and the mirror, as shown in Figs. 3.10 and 3.11 respectively and use the transformation relations
$ egin{aligned}
c k_{x}^{\prime} & =\gamma\left(c k_{x}- eta \omegaight), & c k_{x} & =\gamma\left(c k_{x}^{\prime}+ eta \omega^{\prime}ight) \
c k_{y}^{\prime} & =c k_{y}, & c k_{z}^{\prime} & =c k_{z} \
\omega^{\prime} & =\gamma\left(\omega- eta c k_{x}ight), & \omega & =\gamma\left(\omega^{\prime}+ eta c k_{x}^{\prime}ight)
\end{aligned}$

With $k_{i}= rac{\omega_{i}}{c}= rac{2\pi 
u}{c}, \; k_{r}= rac{\omega_{r}}{c}= rac{2\pi 
u^{\prime}}{c}$, we have for the incident light
$ egin{aligned}
-k_{i}^{\prime} \cos 	heta_{i}^{\prime} & =\gamma\left(-k_{i}\cos 	heta_{i}- rac{ eta\omega_{i}}{c}ight)=-\gamma k_{i}\left(\cos 	heta_{i}+ etaight) \
\omega_{i}^{\prime} & =\gamma\left(\omega_{i}+ eta c k_{i}\cos 	heta_{i}ight)=\gamma \omega_{i}\left(1+ eta\cos 	heta_{i}ight)
\end{aligned}$

or
$k_{i}^{\prime}\cos 	heta_{i}^{\prime}=\gamma k_{i}\left(\cos 	heta_{i}+ etaight), \quad k_{i}^{\prime}=\gamma k_{i}\left(1+ eta\cos 	heta_{i}ight)$.

On reflection, $\omega_{r}^{\prime}=\omega_{i}^{\prime}, \; 	heta_{r}^{\prime}=	heta_{i}^{\prime}$, so for the reflected light we have
$ egin{aligned}
\omega_{r} & =\gamma\left(\omega_{r}^{\prime}+ eta c k_{r}^{\prime}\cos 	heta_{r}^{\prime}ight)=\gamma\left(\omega_{i}^{\prime}+ eta\omega_{i}^{\prime}\cos 	heta_{i}^{\prime}ight), \
k_{r}\cos 	heta_{r} & =\gamma k_{r}^{\prime}\left(\cos 	heta_{r}^{\prime}+ etaight)=\gamma k_{i}^{\prime}\left(\cos 	heta_{i}^{\prime}+ etaight)
\end{aligned}$

or
$ egin{aligned}
k_{r} & =\gamma k_{i}^{\prime}\left(1+ eta\cos 	heta_{i}^{\prime}ight) \
& =\gamma^{2} k_{i}\left(1+ eta\cos 	heta_{i}ight)+\gamma^{2} eta k_{i}\left(\cos 	heta_{i}+ etaight) \
& =\gamma^{2} k_{i}\left(1+2 eta\cos 	heta_{i}+ eta^{2}ight), \
k_{r}\cos 	heta_{r} & =\gamma^{2} k_{i}\left[(1+ eta^{2})\cos 	heta_{i}+2 etaight]
\end{aligned}$

i.e.
$ egin{aligned}

u^{\prime} & = rac{
u\left(1+2 eta\cos 	heta_{i}+ eta^{2}ight)}{1- eta^{2}} \
\cos 	heta_{r} & = rac{(1+ eta^{2})\cos 	heta_{i}+2 eta}{1+2 eta\cos 	heta_{i}+ eta^{2}}
\end{aligned}$
